articles.title: A Blood Type Rarer Than 'Golden Blood'? Meet B(A), Found in Just Three Humans
articles.summary: A Thai hospital's routine blood testing uncovered a genetic anomaly: a blood type called B(A) that only three people worldwide are known to carry. It's mostly B but has trace A antigens, and researchers are hunting for a fourth carrier.

articles.title: A 2,000-Year-Old Crime Scene: Fingerprint in Tar Could Unmask Ancient Raiders
articles.summary: On Scandinavia's oldest plank boat, a partial fingerprint pressed into pine pitch may reveal where seaborne raiders came from when they attacked a Danish island in the 4th century B.C.E. New analysis points to the Baltic Sea, not Germany.

articles.title: The Quantum Twin Paradox: Scientists Will Use Atomic Clocks to Test If Time Flows at Two Speeds at Once
articles.summary: Physicists propose using ultraprecise trapped-ion clocks to test whether time can exist in quantum superposition—flowing fast and slow simultaneously. The experiment could reveal hidden quantum signatures of time-flow, challenging classical physics.
